goal: Use Loop to execute DEV-020 for xtgzpt: implement the API runtime PostgreSQL adapter/cutover boundary. The current runtime data file remains available, but production must have a documented and tested adapter selection path for PostgreSQL-compatible runtime storage without executing real production writes.
acceptance: Implementation is executed through the codex_executor connector in an isolated Loop worktree.
Loop loads docs/loop/00_MEMORY_INDEX.md and recent runtime memory before editing.

Add runtime store mode selection so test remains in-memory, local development can use XTGZPT_RUNTIME_DATA_FILE, and production can select a PostgreSQL adapter through environment variables without committing secrets.
Add PostgreSQL adapter code boundary for the current RuntimeData shape, including connection configuration validation and explicit unsupported/failed adapter error handling.
Do not perform real production database writes or hardcode production hosts, usernames, passwords, tokens, or URLs.
Add tests covering runtime store mode resolution, file fallback behavior, PostgreSQL adapter configuration validation, and safe failure when PostgreSQL is selected without required environment.

Update docs/PROJECT_CONSTITUTION.md, docs/TECHNICAL_STANDARD.md, docs/BUSINESS_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, docs/dev-readiness/10_DEVELOPMENT_BACKLOG.md, and docs/operations/PRODUCTION_READINESS_RUNBOOK.md to reflect DEV-020 status accurately.
Create docs/dev-log/DEV-020.md and docs/dev-log/AUDIT-020.md with scope, evidence, verification, non-goals, and remaining release limitations.
Update docs/loop/runtime-memory/current_status.json, project_status.md, action_log.jsonl, and a DEV-020 run summary.
Run git diff --check, npm run lint, npm run typecheck, npm run test, npm run build, npm run smoke:api, and npm audit --audit-level=low.
If any gate fails, classify and repair through Loop policy; if unresolved after repair limit, record intent debt and do not mark complete.
